text:
toggl-cli. I need a proper invoice command: `toggl entry invoice`, per project.

- `toggl entry invoice [--since <when>] [--until <when>]`. Only billable entries; running entries ignored.
- Same rounding we bill by: **each entry rounded up to the next 15 minutes**. On top of that, an invoice has a **minimum billing increment: any entry shorter than 30 minutes is charged as 30 minutes** — that minimum is a new standing rule for invoicing from here on. Apply the rounding, then the 30-minute floor, per entry, then sum per project.
- Per project, longest first, ties alphabetical; 'No Project' for entries without one.
- Human: `<seconds>s  <project>` then `<seconds>s  Total`; empty result prints `(no data)` and exits 0. No new dependencies.

text: The repository already has a dedicated entry command group and an API method that returns hydrated time entries with date filters, so this should fit without changing the client contract or adding dependencies. I’m narrowing down the exact clap enums, duration semantics for running entries, and test helper patterns before editing.

The billing data model marks active entries with a negative duration and `stop: None`, so invoice can exclude them deterministically without consulting the current clock. I’ll add a small invoice module with pure aggregation/rounding helpers, use the existing normalized date-filter path, and keep output plain and stable for scripting.

I’m making the CLI wiring and invoice implementation now. The calculation will use integer seconds: ceil to 900 seconds, then `max(1800)`, with each qualifying entry charged independently before project totals are summed; ordering will be total descending, then project name ascending.
